forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: cd80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Dropping a file on an attachment-list field crashes with `name.lastIndexOf` TypeError
+---
+## What
+
+Reported behavior: dragging a file into an attachment field shows the global error overlay ("Something went wrong") with a stack trace pointing at `name.lastIndexOf`.
+
+### Root cause
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`
+
+The drop pipeline is:
+
+1. `FileDropProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/file-drop-context.tsx`) handles the HTML5 drop, calls `save_dropped_file` to write the dataTransfer bytes to a temp file, and invokes the topmost registered drop callback with `paths: string[]` (temp paths).
+2. `AttachmentListDisplay`'s drop callback commits `[...current, ...paths]` — i.e. pushes a raw **string** path into an array typed as `AttachmentMeta[]`. This is the documented contract: the entity layer (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/add.rs::process_attachment_field`) accepts string paths in the `attachments` field, copies the file into `.attachments/`, and on next read enriches the entry into a full `AttachmentMeta` (`{ id, name, size, mime_type, path }`).
+3. **Between commit and the read-back**, React re-renders with the array containing a string. `AttachmentListDisplay` does:
+
+   ```tsx
+   const attachments = Array.isArray(value) ? (value as AttachmentMeta[]) : [];
+   // ...
+   {attachments.map((att) => (
+     <AttachmentItem key={att.id} attachment={att} />
+   ))}
+   ```
+
+   The cast hides the fact that `att` may be a string. `AttachmentItem` then calls:
+
+   ```ts
+   getFileIcon(attachment.mime_type, attachment.name)
+   ```
+
+   With `attachment` being a string, both `.mime_type` and `.name` are `undefined`. Inside `getFileIcon`, `getExtension(undefined)` runs `undefined.lastIndexOf(".")` → **TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'lastIndexOf')**. The top-level error boundary surfaces this as "Something went wrong".
+
+### Why the editor doesn't crash
+
+`AttachmentEditor` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/attachment-editor.tsx:132–143`) explicitly branches on `typeof att === "string"` and renders pending paths as plain text. The display does not.
+
+### Single-attachment variant
+
+`AttachmentDisplay` (the non-list variant) does not crash because its `hasAttachment` guard requires `typeof attachment === "object"`, so a string commit is treated as empty and the drop-zone placeholder is shown — but visual feedback is briefly lost during the round-trip. That UX gap is **out of scope** for this task; this task fixes the crash in the list variant only. File a follow-up if the single-display feedback matters.
+
+### Fix
+
+`AttachmentListDisplay` must tolerate string entries during the commit→read round-trip, mirroring the editor's pattern. Two reasonable approaches:
+
+- **Render pending paths inline** (mirrors editor): when an entry is a string, render a row with the file's basename (`path.split('/').pop()`) and a muted style — same shape as `AttachmentItem` so the row doesn't reflow when the metadata arrives.
+- **Filter pending paths out**: only `AttachmentMeta` entries hit `AttachmentItem`. Cleaner code but loses the just-dropped visual.
+
+Recommend the first approach for parity with the editor — users see immediate feedback that the drop landed.
+
+Either way, fixing `getFileIcon`/`getExtension` to defensively handle `undefined` is **not** the right fix on its own — it only papers over the type-cast lie in `AttachmentListDisplay`. Fix the cast, then the helpers can stay strict.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Dropping a file onto an `attachment-list` field no longer throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'lastIndexOf')`.
+- [ ] During the brief window between the drop commit and the entity-layer read-back enrichment, the dropped file is represented in the list (basename or equivalent placeholder) without crashing.
+- [ ] Once the entity-layer round-trip completes and the array contains only `AttachmentMeta` objects, the rendering is identical to the pre-fix happy path (no visual regression on already-saved attachments).
+- [ ] The cast `(value as AttachmentMeta[])` in `AttachmentListDisplay` is replaced with a value shape that reflects the actual mixed `(AttachmentMeta | string)[]` runtime contract.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] **Regression unit test** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.test.tsx`: render `<AttachmentListDisplay mode="full" value={[validMeta, "/tmp/dropped-file.png"]} />` (a mixed array containing one valid `AttachmentMeta` and one string path). Assert that the render does not throw and that both entries are visible in the DOM (the meta by name, the string by its basename).
+- [ ] **Existing test stays green**: the pure-meta render case (`value={[validMeta]}`) still renders via `AttachmentItem` with the existing icon/size layout.
+- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test attachment-display` — all green.
+- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` — no new TS errors from removing the unsafe cast.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the mixed-array regression test first, watch it throw the `lastIndexOf` TypeError, then change `AttachmentListDisplay` so the test passes.

-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] Dropping a file onto an `attachment-list` field no longer throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'lastIndexOf')`.

Assert that the render does not throw and that both entries are visible in the DOM (the meta by name, the string by its basename).
-- [ ] **Existing test stays green**: the pure-meta render case (`value={[validMeta]}`) still renders via `AttachmentItem` with the existing icon/size layout.

changes.value:
## What

Reported behavior: dragging a file into an attachment field shows the global error overlay ("Something went wrong") with a stack trace pointing at `name.lastIndexOf`.

### Root cause

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`

The drop pipeline is:

1. `FileDropProvider` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/file-drop-context.tsx`) handles the HTML5 drop, calls `save_dropped_file` to write the dataTransfer bytes to a temp file, and invokes the topmost registered drop callback with `paths: string[]` (temp paths).
2. `AttachmentListDisplay`'s drop callback commits `[...current, ...paths]` — i.e. pushes a raw **string** path into an array typed as `AttachmentMeta[]`. This is the documented contract: the entity layer (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/attachment/add.rs::process_attachment_field`) accepts string paths in the `attachments` field, copies the file into `.attachments/`, and on next read enriches the entry into a full `AttachmentMeta` (`{ id, name, size, mime_type, path }`).
3. **Between commit and the read-back**, React re-renders with the array containing a string. `AttachmentListDisplay` does:

   ```tsx
   const attachments = Array.isArray(value) ? (value as AttachmentMeta[]) : [];
   // ...
   {attachments.map((att) => (
     <AttachmentItem key={att.id} attachment={att} />
   ))}
   ```

   The cast hides the fact that `att` may be a string. `AttachmentItem` then calls:

   ```ts
   getFileIcon(attachment.mime_type, attachment.name)
   ```

   With `attachment` being a string, both `.mime_type` and `.name` are `undefined`. Inside `getFileIcon`, `getExtension(undefined)` runs `undefined.lastIndexOf(".")` → **TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'lastIndexOf')**. The top-level error boundary surfaces this as "Something went wrong".

### Why the editor doesn't crash

`AttachmentEditor` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/attachment-editor.tsx:132–143`) explicitly branches on `typeof att === "string"` and renders pending paths as plain text. The display does not.

### Single-attachment variant

`AttachmentDisplay` (the non-list variant) does not crash because its `hasAttachment` guard requires `typeof attachment === "object"`, so a string commit is treated as empty and the drop-zone placeholder is shown — but visual feedback is briefly lost during the round-trip. That UX gap is **out of scope** for this task; this task fixes the crash in the list variant only. File a follow-up if the single-display feedback matters.

### Fix

`AttachmentListDisplay` must tolerate string entries during the commit→read round-trip, mirroring the editor's pattern. Two reasonable approaches:

- **Render pending paths inline** (mirrors editor): when an entry is a string, render a row with the file's basename (`path.split('/').pop()`) and a muted style — same shape as `AttachmentItem` so the row doesn't reflow when the metadata arrives.
- **Filter pending paths out**: only `AttachmentMeta` entries hit `AttachmentItem`. Cleaner code but loses the just-dropped visual.

Recommend the first approach for parity with the editor — users see immediate feedback that the drop landed.

Either way, fixing `getFileIcon`/`getExtension` to defensively handle `undefined` is **not** the right fix on its own — it only papers over the type-cast lie in `AttachmentListDisplay`. Fix the cast, then the helpers can stay strict.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Dropping a file onto an `attachment-list` field no longer throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'lastIndexOf')`.
- [ ] During the brief window between the drop commit and the entity-layer read-back enrichment, the dropped file is represented in the list (basename or equivalent placeholder) without crashing.
- [ ] Once the entity-layer round-trip completes and the array contains only `AttachmentMeta` objects, the rendering is identical to the pre-fix happy path (no visual regression on already-saved attachments).
- [ ] The cast `(value as AttachmentMeta[])` in `AttachmentListDisplay` is replaced with a value shape that reflects the actual mixed `(AttachmentMeta | string)[]` runtime contract.

## Tests

- [ ] **Regression unit test** in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.test.tsx`: render `<AttachmentListDisplay mode="full" value={[validMeta, "/tmp/dropped-file.png"]} />` (a mixed array containing one valid `AttachmentMeta` and one string path). Assert that the render does not throw and that both entries are visible in the DOM (the meta by name, the string by its basename).
- [ ] **Existing test stays green**: the pure-meta render case (`value={[validMeta]}`) still renders via `AttachmentItem` with the existing icon/size layout.
- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test attachment-display` — all green.
- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` — no new TS errors from removing the unsafe cast.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the mixed-array regression test first, watch it throw the `lastIndexOf` TypeError, then change `AttachmentListDisplay` so the test passes.
Dropping a file on an attachment-list field crashes with `name.lastIndexOf` TypeError

@@ -71,4 +71,15 @@

 ## Workflow

-- Use `/tdd` — write the mixed-array regression test first, watch it throw the `lastIndexOf` TypeError, then change `AttachmentListDisplay` so the test passes.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the mixed-array regression test first, watch it throw the `lastIndexOf` TypeError, then change `AttachmentListDisplay` so the test passes.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-09 11:34)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx` — `PendingAttachmentItem` uses an inline anonymous prop type `{ path: string }`. Every other component in this file already follows the named-interface convention (`AttachmentItemProps`, `AttachmentItemInnerProps`, `AttachmentDisplayProps`, `AttachmentListDisplayProps`) and the JS/TS review guidelines explicitly require it ("Every component gets a `interface FooProps` co-located above it. No anonymous inline object types... Even for 2-prop components — the named interface is the documentation."). Add `interface PendingAttachmentItemProps { path: string }` co-located with the component to match the file's own convention.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx` `basename()` — degrades silently to an empty string for trailing-separator inputs (`"/foo/bar/"`, lone `"/"` or `"\\"`, or empty `""`). None of these are reachable from `save_dropped_file` temp paths today, but the editor's pending branch falls back to the full `att` text so the user always sees *something*. Consider falling back to the original `path` when the computed basename is empty: `return slash >= 0 && slash < path.length - 1 ? path.slice(slash + 1) : path;`.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.test.tsx` — the new tests cover POSIX paths (`/tmp/...`, `/var/folders/...`) but not Windows backslash paths, even though `basename()` explicitly handles `\\`. Add a one-line case like `value={["C:\\\\Users\\\\me\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Temp\\\\photo.jpg"]}` and assert `photo.jpg` renders, so the Windows branch in `basename()` is exercised.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx` `PendingAttachmentItem` — gives no a11y signal that the row is in a transient/loading state; assistive tech only sees the basename and a paperclip. The visual cues (`italic` + `opacity-60`) are purely sighted-user affordances. The editor's parallel branch (`attachment-editor.tsx:132–143`) has the same gap, so this is a parity-preserving omission rather than a regression — but adding `aria-busy="true"` (and optionally a visually-hidden "pending" sibling) here would set a better precedent and could be mirrored into the editor in a follow-up.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx` — `AttachmentEditor.normalizeAttachments` already validates each element via `isValidElement` (string OR object with string `id`), filtering out unexpected shapes. The display now trusts `Array.isArray(value)` and casts to `Array<AttachmentMeta | string>` without per-element validation, so a stray `null`/`number`/`{ no_id: true }` in the array would still hit `AttachmentItem` and crash inside `getFileIcon`. Consider importing/sharing `normalizeAttachments` (or hoisting it to a small util) so the display and editor agree on the runtime contract — closes the parity gap the comment in this PR explicitly cites.
\ No newline at end of file
